mission.title: slimtoolkit: Console output as json with a flag to control when the console output is free form text and when it's json
mission.description: Console output as json with a flag to control when the console output is free form text and when it's json. Requested by 0+ users.

steps.title: Check current slimtoolkit deployment
steps.description:
Verify your slimtoolkit version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n slimtoolkit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=slimtoolkit
helm list -n slimtoolkit 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working slimtoolkit installation.

steps.title: Review slimtoolkit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant slimtoolkit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n slimtoolkit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=slimtoolkit
kubectl get configmap -n slimtoolkit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=slimtoolkit
```
Need to have a global flag to control the console output mode (`--json-output` or something like that).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Console output as json with a flag to control when the
steps.description:
when are you online? I can give you an overview.

A few notes to get you started in the meantime...

The docker-slim I/O happens using the `ExecutionContext` construct ( https://github.com/docker-slim/docker-slim/blob/master/pkg/app/execontext.go ). The`ExecutionContext.Output.Info()`,
```yaml
xc.Out.Info("params",
				ovars{
					"target.type":   "image",
					"target":        targetRef,
					"continue.mode": continueAfter.Mode,
					"rt.as.user":    doRunTargetAsUser,
					"keep.perms":    doKeepPerms,
					"tags":          strings.Join(outputTags, ","),
				})
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n slimtoolkit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=slimtoolkit
kubectl get events -n slimtoolkit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Console output as json with a flag to control when the" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with slimtoolkit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
